4_steps_of_decreasing_size_v_4_3_2_1_1: 4 steps of decreasing size. V = 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 10 cubes.
a_4_4_base_with_hollow_interior_wall_t: A 4×4 base with hollow interior — wall thickness 1. Counts the wall cubes only.
two_pillars_connected_by_a_deck_v_6_6_: Two pillars connected by a deck — V = 6 + 6 + 5 = 17.
tapering_tower_each_level_smaller_than: Tapering tower. Each level smaller than the last.
a_simple_arch_over_an_opening: A simple arch over an opening.
walls_flat_roof_v_4_4_base_wall_roof: Walls + flat roof. V = 4×4 base wall + roof.
interactive_net_drag_the_slider_to_fol: 🗺 Interactive net — drag the slider to fold the 2D net into a 3D prism
fold_the_net_from_flat_0_to_fully_fold: Fold the net from flat (0) to fully folded (1)
when_the_net_folds_up_completely_you_c: When the net folds up completely, you can see the prism. Surface area is the area of all 6 faces — the wrapping paper.
net_the_6_faces_unfolded_onto_a_2d_lay: 🗺 Net — the 6 faces unfolded onto a 2D layout
fold_the_net_up_and_you_get_the_prism_: Fold the net up and you get the prism. Surface area is the total area of all 6 faces — the wrapping paper or paint you would need.
volume_out_of_range_for_comparison_try: Volume out of range for comparison. Try a smaller prism (V ≤ 1000).
volume_grows_as_the_cube_of_the_dimens: volume grows as the cube of the dimension; surface area grows as the square. Same V, very different SA.
a_cube_is_the_most_surface_efficient_p: A cube is the most surface-efficient prism shape.
it_is_why_elephants_need_thick_legs_vo: It is why elephants need thick legs (volume → mass scales faster than bone cross-section),
why_cells_stay_small_need_surface_area: why cells stay small (need surface area to absorb nutrients), and why insulation works (rounder shapes keep heat).
tap_any_row_above_to_swap_the_3d_prism: 👆 Tap any row above to swap the 3D prism to that shape — see the same volume in a different skin.
slider_v_l_w_h_watch_it_grow: Slider — V = l × w × h, watch it grow
sliders_snap_to_integer_dimensions_gre: Sliders snap to integer dimensions — great for the early visual: doubling each side multiplies volume by 8 (2³). Surface area scales as 2²; volume as 2³. The square-cube law explains why elephants have thick legs and shrews can fall safely.
freeform_build_any_shape_count_cubes: Freeform — build any shape, count cubes
l_blocks_hollow_shapes_irregular_prism: L-blocks, hollow shapes, irregular prisms. Volume = total cubes regardless of arrangement. CCSS 5.MD.5: relate volume of a right rectangular prism to multiplication and addition (V = b×h or as the sum of partial volumes).
word_problems_volume_in_the_real_world: Word Problems — volume in the real world
a_fish_tank_with_5_3_4_60_cubes_of_wat: A fish tank with 5×3×4 = 60 cubes of water. A lunchbox of 4×3×2 = 24 sandwich cubes. Every multiplication of three dimensions is a real container with a real capacity. CCSS 5.MD.5b: solve real-world problems involving volume.
pick_a_context_to_make_volume_real: 🌍 Pick a context to make volume real:
the_rectangular_prism_above_shows_exac: 💡 The rectangular prism above shows exactly what the words describe. Volume is the count of unit cubes that fit inside.
adjust_the_sliders_below_to_match_the_: Adjust the sliders below to match the story — or try a new context. Solve 5 word problems to earn 📝 Word Wizard. Visit all 8 contexts for 🌍 Real-World Explorer.
click_grid_to_place_cubes_click_cube_t: 👉 Click grid to place cubes • Click cube to remove • Click above to stack
interactive_3d_viewport_drag_swipe_or_: Interactive 3D viewport. Drag, swipe, or use arrow keys to rotate. Pinch or +/- to zoom. R to reset view.
visual_is_a_voxel_approximation_built_: ⚠ Visual is a voxel approximation (built from unit cubes). The formula
slice_horizontally_to_see_the_area_tim: Slice horizontally to see the area-times-depth relationship
square_units_volume_cross_section_area: square units. Volume = (cross-section area) × (depth) is the foundation of integral calculus.
adjust_your_predicted_dimensions_compa: Adjust your PREDICTED dimensions, compare to actual prism. Discrete outcome: close/mid/far. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_how_do_you_build_intuition_: Hypothesis: How do you build intuition for predicting volume?
which_dimension_affects_volume_the_mos: Which dimension affects volume the most?
try_halving_one_dimension_what_happens: Try halving one dimension — what happens to volume?
when_are_predictions_hardest_when_easi: When are predictions hardest? When easiest?
i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
explain_how_each_dimension_contributes: Explain how each dimension contributes to total volume.
design_note_discrete_3_state_outcome_n: Design note: discrete 3-state outcome; no exact-volume score; no reveal — by design.
buildable_challenges_pick_a_structure_: 📚 Buildable challenges — pick a structure to try
click_a_name_to_set_it_as_your_target_: Click a name to set it as your target — the cubes will appear as ghost outlines. Match the shape, then Check.
reveal_the_solution_gives_up_no_badge: Reveal the solution (gives up — no badge)
save_current_dims_cubes_with_a_name: Save current dims + cubes with a name
export_current_construction_as_png_ima: Export current construction as PNG image
download_a_png_snapshot_of_the_current: Download a PNG snapshot of the current build
nothing_saved_yet_build_something_and_: Nothing saved yet. Build something and click 💾 Save.
